name: variables
context: Terraform input variables, local values, and outputs — the data flow layer in HCL configurations
info: This skill covers the three mechanisms for data flow in Terraform: input variables (parameterize configs), local values (compute intermediate results), and output values (export results). Input variables support type constraints (string, number, bool, list, map, object), default values, descriptions, validation blocks with custom conditions, and the sensitive flag. Local values reduce repetition and compute derived values. Outputs export resource attributes for use by other modules, the CLI, or remote state consumers. Variable precedence follows a defined order: environment variables (TF_VAR_*), terraform.tfvars, *.auto.tfvars, -var-file flags, -var flags, then defaults. See [[terraform/hcl/expressions]] for how to reference variables in expressions.
triggers: how to use locals in terraform

sections.title: Input variables
sections.body: Declared with `variable` blocks. Attributes: `type` (type constraint), `default` (fallback value), `description` (documentation), `sensitive` (redact from output), `nullable` (allow null), `validation` (custom rules). Reference with `var.<name>`. Variables without defaults are required — Terraform prompts for them interactively or they must be supplied via tfvars, CLI flags, or environment variables. Types include `string`, `number`, `bool`, `list(type)`, `set(type)`, `map(type)`, `object({...})`, `tuple([...])`, and `any`.

sections.title: Local values
sections.body: Defined in `locals` blocks. Locals give names to expressions for reuse and readability. Common patterns: name prefixes (`"${var.project}-${var.env}"`), shared tag maps, boolean flags (`var.environment == "prod"`), conditional values, and flattened nested structures. Reference with `local.<name>`. Multiple `locals` blocks are allowed and merged. Locals cannot reference themselves (no recursion).

sections.title: Output values
sections.body: Declared with `output` blocks. Attributes: `value` (required expression), `description`, `sensitive` (redact from CLI), `depends_on` (explicit dependencies). Outputs are shown after `terraform apply`, queryable via `terraform output [-json]`, and accessible to parent modules as `module.<name>.<output>`. Root module outputs are stored in state and can be read by other configurations via `terraform_remote_state` data source.

sections.title: Variable precedence
sections.body: Terraform evaluates variable values in order (last wins): (1) default value in variable block, (2) environment variables with TF_VAR_ prefix, (3) terraform.tfvars auto-loaded file, (4) *.auto.tfvars files in alphabetical order, (5) -var-file flags in command order, (6) -var flags in command order. This lets you layer defaults, environment config, and CLI overrides predictably.
